title: verbascum bracteosum
intro: Verbascum bracteosum, also known as the tall mullein or candelabra mullein, is a biennial plant native to North America, characterized by its tall spikes of yellow flowers and large, fuzzy leaves. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for centuries, valued for its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs. The health benefits of Verbascum bracteosum include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting relaxation, making it a popular choice for natural remedies. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used topically in creams and ointments to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
